label: Opsgenie
description: Manage incidents, alerts, and on-call schedules in Atlassian Opsgenie.

form.title: Connect Opsgenie
form.subtitle: Paste your Opsgenie API key to let Anton create alerts, manage incidents, and query on-call schedules.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste an Opsgenie API integration key from Settings → Integration list → API. Quickest path — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate an Opsgenie API Key

1. Log in to your Opsgenie account at [app.opsgenie.com](https://app.opsgenie.com) (or your Atlassian-hosted URL).
2. Click **Settings** in the left sidebar.
3. Go to **Integration list** (under the Integrations section).
4. Click **Add new integration** in the top-right corner.
5. Search for **API** in the integration list and select it.
6. Give the integration a name like `Anton`.
7. Set the **Access rights** (Read, Create, Update, Delete) based on what you want Anton to do — for full access, enable all.
8. Click **Save integration**.
9. Copy the **API Key** shown on the integration detail page.
10. Paste it into the **API Key** field below.

## Notes

- If your account is on the **EU region**, switch the **Region** dropdown below to `EU` — the base URL differs (`api.eu.opsgenie.com` vs `api.opsgenie.com`).
- The API key is tied to this integration; you can revoke it anytime by deleting the integration in Opsgenie.
- Team-scoped integrations (created inside a Team's integration list) restrict the key to that team's alerts only — use the global integration list for full access.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Copied from Settings → Integration list → API → your integration. Treat this like a password.

fields.name: region
fields.label: Region
fields.description: Select the region that matches your Opsgenie account. US uses api.opsgenie.com; EU uses api.eu.opsgenie.com.
